label: ponder: skill-command-library
prompt:
You are running a ponder session for the idea 'skill-command-library' in the sdlc workspace.

The owner (jordan) has seeded this session with the following message — include it verbatim at the top of your session log as `**jordan · Owner**\nwe want to add ALL commands to our /docs/commands page, follow recommendations based on convo`:

> we want to add ALL commands to our /docs/commands page, follow recommendations based on convo

## Step 1 — Load context

```bash
sdlc ponder show skill-command-library
sdlc ponder session list skill-command-library
```

Read the manifest, every scrapbook artifact, and the most recent session log (`sdlc ponder session read skill-command-library <N>`) to restore full context. Load team member agent definitions from .claude/agents/. Orient from the orientation strip (WHERE WE ARE / NEXT MOVE / COMMIT SIGNAL).

## Step 2 — Run the session

You are a facilitator. Channel recruited thought partners by name — voice their perspectives, let them push back, surface tensions. Interrogate the brief: push past stated solutions to find real problems. Capture insights as scrapbook artifacts:
```bash
sdlc ponder capture skill-command-library --content "<markdown>" --as <name>.md
```
Use inline markers: `⚑  Decided:` for resolved points, `?  Open:` for live tensions.

## Step 3 — Log the session (MANDATORY)

Before ending, you MUST log the session. This is not optional — skipping it means the session is invisible to the web UI and to future agents.

The ONLY correct procedure:
1. Write the complete session Markdown to a temp file using the Write tool:
`/tmp/ponder-session-skill-command-library.md`
2. Run: `sdlc ponder session log skill-command-library --file /tmp/ponder-session-skill-command-library.md`

This command auto-numbers the file, places it in the `sessions/` subdirectory, increments the session counter, and mirrors orientation to the manifest.
